title: Probabilistic Models of Indexing and Searching
why_notable: Longest recognition lag in current 250-paper table; useful for explaining the long tail of delayed recognition.
careful_wording: Can be described as an extreme long-lag example in the current award-seed dataset; do not infer the exact award motivation without citation reading.

title: The ALOHA system
why_notable: Very early networking paper with 32-year recognition lag and high citation count relative to other long-lag cases.
careful_wording: Can support the claim that some foundational systems/networking work remains visible decades later; avoid claiming industrial deployment solely from this table.

title: Collaborative Filtering for Implicit Feedback Datasets
why_notable: Shortest observed lag category and high citation count; good contrast against 30-year long-tail cases.
careful_wording: Can be framed as a fast-recognition case within the award mechanism; still not evidence of immediate impact in publication year.

title: gSpan: Graph-Based Substructure Pattern Mining
why_notable: Another 9-year lag case with substantial citation depth and breadth proxy
careful_wording: useful for showing short-lag cases are not isolated.
human_check: Can be described as a short-window data mining example; do not rank it above other papers by value using citation count alone.

title: Object Recognition from Local Scale-Invariant Features
why_notable: Recognition lag equals the dataset median of 12 years while citation count is among the highest in the table.
careful_wording: Can bridge Time and Citation modules: a typical recognition window can still contain very high-impact methods; citation count is not award cause.

title: XORs in the air: practical wireless network coding
why_notable: Median-lag networking case used in the Time module; useful as a stable demo example.
careful_wording: Can be used to explain the typical decade-plus Test-of-Time window; final report should verify the exact contribution description.
